title: Orbital Phase Radiometry
content:
Rhea Jardine led a research group at the Mossenden Technical Institute in Tallmere that advanced orbital phase radiometry. The group's foundational results were published in 1885.

Theory.

The laboratory released open calibration datasets that other sites adopted within months.

Apparatus.

Criticism focused on sampling bias when instruments were operated without controlled humidity shields.

Methods.

Pilot studies in 1883 demonstrated stability improvements over earlier thermal imaging pipelines.

Results.

Peer review highlighted reproducibility across three independent sites, including a winter trial in Tallmere.
